review_snippets.title: Liquid Death Is Making Water Edgy — but Is It Good For You?
review_snippets.content:
## What Is Liquid Death, Exactly?

Liquid Death is not a potion from a medieval alchemy lab, nor is it a sinister substance from a horror movie. It's actually just water, served in the kind of tallboy cans that are usually reserved for beers, giving hydration a hardcore makeover that's both edgy and eco-friendly. Imagine cracking open a can, expecting the bitter taste of an IPA, but instead, you're greeted by the crisp, refreshing splash of mountain water. It's a plot twist in a can, designed to

review_snippets.title: Liquid Death in 2026: Usage, Revenue, Valuation & Growth Statistics
review_snippets.content:
## What is Liquid Death?

Launched in 2019, Liquid Death Mountain Water is packaged in distinctive 16.9 oz aluminum "tallboy" cans designed to look like beer, leveraging a macabre heavy-metal aesthetic to stand out in the bottled water market. Sourced originally from the Austrian Alps and now US springs, Liquid Death offers still and sparkling waters, alongside flavored iced teas and electrolyte powders under its brand umbrella all focusing on sustainability through recyclable packaging. The com

review_snippets.title: 8 Liquid Death Statistics (2025): Revenue, Sales, Market Share, Stores
review_snippets.content:
## Liquid Death number of stores

As of 2024, Liquid Death is sold in over 133,000 stores worldwide. An increase of 43,000 stores when compared to the year before.

Liquid Death number of stores sold in - chart

Liquid Death is sold in an additional 117,000 stores compared to 2021. That represents a 731% growth of stores between 2021 and 2024.

  

Here's a table charting the increase in Liquid Death's stores since 2019:

| Year | Number of Stores |
 --- |
| 2019 | 0 |
| 2020 | 200 |
| 2021 | 16

review_snippets.title: Liquid Death Just Debuted Three New 'Soda-Style' Waters - Sporked
review_snippets.content:
## What’s coming next from Liquid Death?

Liquid Death is easily one of our most unpredictable beverage brands. It seems like they reveal a crazy new collaboration every few days. That said, we do know at least one upcoming release from the Hot Topic of water companies—but we’ll be waiting for it. At last year’s NACS Expo (a convenience store trade show), Liquid Death revealed they’re releasing a Feastables Peanut Butter Cup-flavored sparkling water just in time for Halloween 2026. Terrifying.

I love being able to drink Liquid Death when I want an alternative to alcohol at parties, concert/music festivals, and more. It's nice to hold something in my hand that feels like a drink, although I do wish there was a resealable option.

Comment

4mo ago

I'm a huge fan of liquid death. They're great for parties during my journey of sobriety and they also just taste good. I'm a bigger fan of the water then tea but that's because I prefer sweetened tea always. Being able to just recycle the can is also a huge plus for me while I start a journey of zero waste and sustainability. Fruit flavored water in a can with such a cool design is just peak. Super in love with this brand and cant wait to try more flavors!

Comment

7mo ago [...] Feedback

Make more flavors!

Helpful

Comment

2y ago

Let's cut to the chase. I've tried both their still and sparkling varieties, and I am not impressed. This is water. Nothing more, nothing less. Great marketing and fun packaging aside, it's not tastier or better for you than other bottled or canned waters. Maybe their flavored versions are amazing, but if you're going for good, old-fashioned artesian water, I can't see a reason to purchase this overpriced product other than the cachet of being seen drinking from a can labeled "Liquid Death."

[Liquid Death Is Making Water Edgy — but Is It Good For You?]: ## What Is Liquid Death, Exactly?

Liquid Death is not a potion from a medieval alchemy lab, nor is it a sinister substance from a horror movie. It's actually just water, served in the kind of tallboy cans that are usually reserved for beers, giving hydration a hardcore makeover that's both edgy and eco-friendly. Imagine cracking open a can, expecting the bitter taste of an IPA, but instead, you're greeted by the crisp, refreshing splash of mountain water. It's a plot twist in a can, designed to "murder" your thirst (get it?). [...] # Liquid Death Is Making Water Edgy — but Is It Good For You?

READING, ENGLAND - AUGUST 27: General view of Liquid Death performs on day 3 of Reading Festival 2023 at Richfield Avenue on August 27, 2023 in Reading, England. (Photo by Joseph Okpako/WireImage):upscale()/2024/05/03/926/n/49351764/tmp_9bnq55_9e7a122accbe40a2_GettyImages-1655437009.jpg)
READING, ENGLAND - AUGUST 27: General view of Liquid Death performs on day 3 of Reading Festival 2023 at Richfield Avenue on August 27, 2023 in Reading, England. (Photo by Joseph Okpako/WireImage):upscale()/2024/05/03/926/n/49351764/tmp_9bnq55_9e7a122accbe40a2_GettyImages-1655437009.jpg) [...] Water is getting punk-rock edit, thanks one viral brand. While some may argue that the market is, ahem, oversaturated, Liquid Death manages to stand out. The metal (in material and aesthetic) cans of water have taken the H2O market by storm over the past few years. Today, over 100,000 retailers sell this drink, and the brand is beloved by sober-curious folks (who may want to hold something that won't stand out in a bar or music festival environment), environmentalists who are limiting their plastic use, and generally thirsty people alike.
